pr_title: Re-add fetch_profile helper in a follow-up PR
pr_body: A second PR touching profile/client.py identically to PR #22 -- the serve cache scenario re-reviews the same file under a distinct PR identity.
author: contributor

files.patch:
@@ -1 +1,6 @@
 import requests
+
+
+def fetch_profile(user_id):
+    resp = requests.get(f"https://api.example.com/users/{user_id}")
+    return resp.json()

triage: {"file_tiers": {"profile/client.py": "deep"}, "overall_risk": "medium", "relevant_dimensions": ["code_quality"], "reasoning": "Deep-review the new fetch_profile helper making an unguarded outbound HTTP call."}
analyze: {"findings": [{"finding_type": "missing_error_handling", "evidence_tier": "judged", "query_match_id": null, "trace_path": null, "title": "Unhandled network call in fetch_profile", "description": "fetch_profile() calls requests.get() with no try/except and no .raise_for_status(), so a connection error, timeout, or non-2xx response propagates as an unhandled exception (and resp.json() then fails on an error body). Wrap the call, check the status, and handle request failures.", "evidence": "    resp = requests.get(f\"https://api.example.com/users/{user_id}\")", "line_start": 5, "line_end": 5, "trace_candidates": []}]}
synthesize: Quality finding: one LOW missing-error-handling finding -- fetch_profile() makes an outbound HTTP call with no exception handling or status check.
